description:
Pattern library catalog (#269). Bridges the per-axis extractor outputs (#264-#272) to substrate-native Loom primitives. Each entry maps a recognizable reference-site compositional pattern to its preferred substrate counterpart. The mapping engine (#273) walks this catalog to translate extracted signals into a SiteSpec.

Design principle: opinionated translation. SaaS tropes in the reference get mapped to their editorial counterparts (feature_spotlight 3-col → kv_pair, stat_band → sparkline, testimonial-card → pull_quote). Per docs/REFERENCE_MATCHING.md.

patterns.name: Centered single-line SaaS hero
patterns.notes: When the reference shows a single-line centered hero, the mapping engine emits hero_editorial with an asymmetric layout and a monospace kicker — editorial counterpart, not faithful copy. Per editorial_purity_gate.

patterns.name: Asymmetric editorial hero
patterns.notes: Already substrate-native; mapping engine accepts the reference's choice.

patterns.name: Split hero with code visual
patterns.notes: Technical-product tier signature. Stripe / Linear / Vercel.

patterns.name: Feature spotlight 3-column grid (SaaS trope)
patterns.notes: Per editorial_purity_gate trope dictionary: the 3-column icon-tile grid maps to kv_pair dense info panels.

patterns.name: Stat band — 'Numbers that' (SaaS trope)
patterns.notes: Per editorial_purity_gate: the 99.99% / 10M+ stat band maps to per-metric sparklines or histograms. Editorial signal, not marketing pressure.

patterns.name: Pricing with highlighted-tier badge (SaaS trope)
patterns.notes: Pricing maps to a tier-vs-feature kv_pair table without the highlighted-tier upsell badge. Per pricing_editorial template.

patterns.name: Testimonial card with avatar (SaaS trope)
patterns.notes: Per editorial_purity_gate: avatar-circle testimonial card maps to editorial pull_quote with body + plain attribution.

patterns.name: Cookie notice — dismiss-only (GDPR violation)
patterns.notes: Per editorial_purity_gate: the reject button must be as prominent as accept. Mapping engine rewrites with a full reject_label.

patterns.name: Marquee logo wall — 'Trusted by'
patterns.notes: Static logo grid instead of scrolling marquee; editorial counterpart.

patterns.name: CTA band with gradient + dual buttons (SaaS trope)
patterns.notes: Drop gradient backdrop + reduce to single-CTA; editorial restraint.

notes: Each pattern entry's `trope: true` marks it as a SaaS shape that gets translated to a substrate-native counterpart. Per docs/EDITORIAL_PURITY.md.
`source_signals` is a sketch — actual mapping (#273) uses extractor output to evaluate. Keys here describe the SHAPE that triggers the pattern, not a strict JSON Schema.
`substrate_target` is the slug of the substrate-native CmsSection kind the mapping emits.
`substrate_variant_hint` is an optional variant string passed through to the synthesized SectionSpec.
`rejected_alternatives` documents which CmsSection kinds the engine refuses to emit even when extractor confidence is high — preserves the editorial-purity contract.
Per [[backward-compat-version-discipline]]: spec=v1. New patterns land additive; renaming an id breaks the mapping engine.
